title: Start a Compost Bucket
description: Build a realistic compost bucket using hydroponic trimmings and kitchen scraps.

dialogue.text: Waste not, friend! We'll build a real compost bucket and turn plant scraps into future soil food.

options.text: I am in. Walk me through it.
Skip to the compost quiz

dialogue.text: First UX tip: in DSPACE, crafting processes live on item pages. Open Inventory → the required item, then scroll to its Process section to run the action.

options.text: Where exactly do I find the required item pages?

dialogue.text: You need a 5 gallon bucket and gloves. In Inventory, open each item page, then check the Process section for "Vent and layer a kitchen compost bucket." The process card shows required inputs and the output item before you run it.

options.text: Got it. What inputs should I gather first?

dialogue.text: For realistic compost we need greens + browns. Greens here are hydroponic trimmings: bundle of basil leaves and bundle of stevia leaves. Browns are dry paper/cardboard you stage mentally while we track the organic inputs as items.

options.text: Teach me the science with a quick quiz
I have bucket, gloves, and hydroponic trimmings ready
Nitrogen-rich fresh material like food scraps and green trimmings
Only dry cardboard and wood chips
Sterile gravel to improve drainage
Damp like a wrung-out sponge
Bone-dry so mold cannot form
Completely saturated with standing water

dialogue.text: Quiz 3: Why turn/aerate a compost bucket?

options.text: To keep oxygen flowing for aerobic microbes and reduce odors
To cool it down below freezing
Because anaerobic slime is faster and safer

dialogue.text: Quiz 4: Which material should stay OUT of this beginner bucket?

options.text: Meat, oily food, or dairy that attracts pests
Shredded paper and dry leaves
Vegetable and herb trimmings

dialogue.text: Quiz 5: What core temperature usually signals active hot composting?

options.text: Roughly 43-65 C (110-150 F) once the pile is established
0-5 C (32-41 F) for peak microbial activity
Above 95 C (203 F) nonstop

dialogue.text: Nice attempt. Compost works best with balanced greens and browns, oxygen, moderate moisture, and pest-safe inputs. Retry the quiz and lock it in.

options.text: Retry quiz

dialogue.text: Great. Now open the 5 gallon bucket item page and run the process in its Process section. Confirm the required inputs include basil and stevia leaf bundles, then run it and watch for the layered compost bucket output item.

options.text: Run process from the bucket item page
Process complete: I have a layered compost bucket

dialogue.text: Perfect start. Keep feeding the bucket small chopped scraps, cover each green layer with dry browns, and aerate on schedule. You earned compost monitoring gear plus a finished blend sample for your garden loop.

options.text: Compost bucket started
